flashcards.front: What is a 'pattern' in the context of pattern classification?
flashcards.back: An n-dimensional vector of feature measurements representing an object or event. It is a point in an n-dimensional feature space.

flashcards.front: What is the fundamental rule of the Minimum Distance Classifier (MDC)?
flashcards.back: Assign an unknown pattern to the class whose prototype (e.g., mean vector) is closest to it, as measured by a distance metric (e.g., Euclidean distance).

flashcards.front: Name two common features extracted from a segmented object for classification.
flashcards.back: Any two of: Area, Perimeter, Compactness, Moment Invariants, Texture descriptors.

flashcards.front: What is the primary disadvantage of using the simple Euclidean MDC?
flashcards.back: It assumes classes form spherical clusters of similar size. It fails for classes with complex, non-spherical distributions.

flashcards.front: What is the role of morphological operations in the pattern classification pipeline?
flashcards.back: To pre-process and clean segmented images (remove noise, smooth boundaries, separate touching objects) to create better-defined regions for accurate feature extraction.
